question: Daniel is packing crates of tires for storage in a warehouse. Each crate can hold 9 tires. If Daniel has 469 tires to store and fills all but the last crate, how many full crates of tires will Daniel have to put in the warehouse?

solution.strategy: Divide the total number of tires by how many fit in each crate.
solution.final_answer: 52 full crates and 1 additional crate holding 1 tire.
